document_info.title: Session Programme: Marking and Intercepting to Stop Goals
document_info.topic: Defensive Positioning, Individual Marking, and Team Interceptions

curriculum_overview.week_4: Be on guard to intercept revisited
curriculum_overview.week_6: Front to back, defend to attack

objectives: To become positive and enthusiastic defenders[cite: 23]
Use scanning, movement and positioning skills when marking as an individual[cite: 23]
Understand the role of covering and communication when defending in pairs[cite: 23]
coaching_points: Adapt movement to track runs and the movement of the opponent[cite: 23].
Scan effectively to make good decisions about marking an opponent or space[cite: 23].
Use a side-on body position when marking tight and covering spaces[cite: 23].
Use quick movement to get into a covering position[cite: 23].
Mirror first defender's body position to enable effective scanning[cite: 23].
Give early and clear communication to the first defender[cite: 23].

activities.name: Diamond Gate Tag

pitch_diagram.image_description: A 10x10-yard grid arranged in a diamond shape on a green field backdrop. Each tip of the diamond features two small yellow cones placed closely together to establish four distinct gates. A queue of four blue players stands ready at the bottom apex, while four yellow-striped players stand at the top apex. White dashed lines loop around the lateral gates, depicting prospective curved running and tracking movements from the base up toward the flanks.

tactical_layout.dimensions: 10x15-yard area configured as a vertical diamond[cite: 23]
tactical_layout.player_positions: Attackers line up at the bottom vertex while defenders line up directly opposite at the top vertex[cite: 23].

activities.description: Set up a 10x10-yard diamond-shape. Put two cones down at each point to create four gates[cite: 23]. Have up to four attackers behind a gate and four defenders behind the gate opposite[cite: 23]. An attacker shouts 'go' and runs to get through another gate[cite: 23]. At the same time, a defender tries to tag them[cite: 23]. Players get a point for getting through a side gate and two points for getting through the defenders' gate[cite: 23]. After all three attackers have had a go, the teams swap roles[cite: 23].

activities.name: Parallel Pitch Grids (End Zones vs Gate Protection)

pitch_diagram.image_description: Two separate 20x15-yard rectangular fields displayed side-by-side. The left field contains shaded blue end zones along both baselines, with a blue player making a lateral pass into the target grid area where a blue teammate controls it under pressure from a yellow-striped challenger. The right field is laid out cleanly with a white horizontal centerline dividing the pitch, while yellow cone gates protect the end lines.

tactical_layout.left_pitch_mechanics: Features shaded baseline end zones for point triggers[cite: 23].
tactical_layout.right_pitch_mechanics: Features small cone gates placed symmetrically along both baselines[cite: 23].

activities.description: Create parallel 20x15-yard pitches for 3v3 or 4v4 games[cite: 23]. Have end zones on the first pitch[cite: 23]. Here, teams score a point for dribbling the ball into an end zone[cite: 23]. They get two points if they pass the ball into the zone for a teammate to control[cite: 23]. The receiver can only be in there for five seconds[cite: 23]. On the other pitch, have two gates at each end with a goalkeeper behind them[cite: 23]. Teams get a point for passing the ball through a gate[cite: 23]. They get two points if they pass through a gate for the keeper to receive[cite: 23]. Passes need to be below shoulder height[cite: 23].

activities.name: Feeder-Attacker Goal Isolation

pitch_diagram.image_description: A vertical pitch grid containing a single full-sized goal box at the top baseline guarded by a grey goalkeeper avatar. Inside the lower half, a blue feeder player initiates a long diagonal ball forward to an attacking blue teammate. A yellow-striped defender is tightly pinned against the attacker's back, using a low, side-on stance to block a direct avenue toward the penalty arc.

tactical_layout.equipment: 1 full-sized goal frame with a dynamic goalkeeper[cite: 23]
tactical_layout.player_positions: 1 deep attacking feeder serving an active striker who is being man-marked 1v1 by a central defender[cite: 23].

activities.description: Set up multiple 20x15-yard pitches with a goal at one end[cite: 23]. Have an attacker, defender and a feeder[cite: 23]. Add a goalkeeper if using groups of four[cite: 23]. The feeder passes to the attacker and they try to score[cite: 23]. The defender needs to mark the attacker and force them away from goal[cite: 23]. The attacker can use the feeder player as a passing option if needed[cite: 23]. After three goes, all players rotate positions and the practice restarts[cite: 23].

activities.name: Parallel Match Progression with Score Tracker Overload

pitch_diagram.image_description: An advanced operational modification of the double-pitch setup. On the right pitch, the baseline gates have been entirely replaced with regular mini-goal targets. Small whiteboard tracking icons are plotted at the upper far corners of the layout, with a blue player avatar shown running completely off the playing field toward a whiteboard to update the tally.

tactical_layout.scoring_mechanics: Scoring a goal forces the scorer to leave the perimeter boundaries to manually log the point, triggering a brief numerical underload/overload phase on the field[cite: 23].

activities.description: Return to the parallel games[cite: 23]. But this time, replace the gates with goals on the second pitch[cite: 23]. Add the rule for both pitches: when a goal is scored, the scorer runs off the pitch to record the goal on a whiteboard or using cones as counters[cite: 23]. This provides an overload for a short time[cite: 23].

objectives: To become positive and enthusiastic defenders[cite: 23]
Use scanning, movement and positioning skills when marking as an individual[cite: 23]
Understand the role of covering and communication when defending in pairs[cite: 23]
coaching_points: Adapt movement to track runs and the movement of the opponent[cite: 23].
Scan effectively to make good decisions about marking an opponent or space[cite: 23].
Use a side-on body position when marking tight and covering spaces[cite: 23].
Use quick movement to get into a covering position[cite: 23].
Mirror first defender's body position to enable effective scanning[cite: 23].
Give early and clear communication to the first defender[cite: 23].

activities.name: Diamond Gate Passing Entry

pitch_diagram.image_description: A replica of the 10x10-yard diamond gate framework from week 1. However, the operational setup shows the ball starting with the yellow-striped defender at the top vertex instead of the attacker[cite: 23]. A dashed horizontal passing vector shows the defender passing the ball directly into the chest of the blue attacker at the base to initialize the duel[cite: 23].

tactical_layout.starting_action: Defender delivers a service pass to the attacker to start the live dribbling challenge[cite: 23].

activities.description: Set up a 10x10-yard diamond-shape. Put two cones down at each point to create four gates[cite: 23]. Have up to four attackers behind a gate and four defenders behind the gate opposite[cite: 23]. This time, a defender starts the game by passing to the attacker[cite: 23]. The attacker then tries to dribble through a gate[cite: 23]. Attackers get a point for getting through a side gate and two points for getting through the defenders' gate[cite: 23]. Defenders need to stop them from reaching any gate[cite: 23]. After all three attackers have had a go, the teams swap roles[cite: 23]. This can be progressed to a 2v1 or 2v2 activity[cite: 23].

activities.name: End Zones vs 4-Goal Grid Match

pitch_diagram.image_description: A structural replica of the double-pitch layout from week 1. The left pitch retains the shared baseline end zones with matching blue and yellow configurations. The right pitch template is updated to feature two mini-goals placed flat on each baseline instead of passing gates, creating a four-goal game format guarded by goalkeepers.

tactical_layout.left_pitch_mechanics: Dribbling or passing into targeted end zones to execute control sequences[cite: 23].
tactical_layout.right_pitch_mechanics: A four-goal configuration featuring twin mini-goals anchored on both baselines[cite: 23].

activities.description: Create parallel 20x15-yard pitches for 3v3 or 4v4 games[cite: 23]. Have end zones on the first pitch[cite: 23]. Here, teams score a point for dribbling the ball into an end zone[cite: 23]. They get two points if they pass the ball into the zone for a teammate to control[cite: 23]. The receiver can only be in there for five seconds[cite: 23]. On the other pitch, play a four-goal game[cite: 23]. Put two mini-goals at each end with a goalkeeper[cite: 23]. Teams get a point for each goal they score[cite: 23]. If mini-goals aren't available, use cones to create gates for the keepers to protect[cite: 23].

activities.name: Feeder-Attacker Goal Isolation (Revisited)

pitch_diagram.image_description: A complete matching visual of the week 1 practice 2 layout. Outfielders track identical positional roles within the 20x15-yard vertical channel model under a 1v1 plus goalkeeper model.

tactical_layout.player_positions: 1 deep server passing forward to an isolated attacker marked tight by a trailing defender[cite: 23].

activities.description: Set up multiple 20x15-yard pitches with a goal at one end[cite: 23]. Have an attacker, defender and a feeder[cite: 23]. Add a goalkeeper if using groups of four[cite: 23]. The feeder passes to the attacker and they try to score[cite: 23]. The defender needs to mark the attacker and force them away from goal[cite: 23]. The attacker can use the feeder player as a passing option if needed[cite: 23]. After three goes, all players rotate positions and the practice restarts[cite: 23].

activities.name: Score Tracker Overload (Revisited)

pitch_diagram.image_description: A match visual mirroring the week 1 practice 3 progression layout. Whiteboard and tally boxes monitor team counts on the sidelines, forcing the scoring individual to temporarily leave the playing parameters.

tactical_layout.scoring_mechanics: Scoring triggers a temporary numerical asymmetry while the scorer logs the point externally[cite: 23].

activities.description: Return to the parallel games[cite: 23]. But this time, replace the gates with goals on the second pitch[cite: 23]. Add the rule for both pitches: when a goal is scored, the scorer runs off the pitch to record the goal on a whiteboard or using cones as counters[cite: 23]. This provides an overload for a short time[cite: 23].

objectives: Show intelligence when making decisions as defenders[cite: 23]
Add timing and deception skills when marking as an individual[cite: 23]
Understand the core skill of intercepting when defending in pairs and units[cite: 23]
coaching_points: Consolidate coaching points from the first two weeks[cite: 23].
When intercepting, judge whether it's possible by trying to see path of the ball and nearby attackers[cite: 23].
Decide to use 'best' surface for making first contact on the ball[cite: 23].
Execute an interception with a positive first touch[cite: 23].

activities.name: On Guard Gate Blocking

pitch_diagram.image_description: A 10x10-yard square area featuring four small double-cone gates mapped out in the center of each perimeter boundary line. A lone yellow-striped attacker operates inside the central grid holding a ball. Three blue defenders stand wide on the exterior boundaries, using scanning and lateral tracking paths to cut off and block access to the gates. White dashed lines indicate prospective escaping and tracking paths.

tactical_layout.player_positions: 1 active central attacker holding the ball vs. 3 shifting defenders managing the perimeter gates from the outside[cite: 23].

activities.description: For 'on guard', set up four gates in a 10x10-yard area[cite: 23]. Have an attacker with a ball in the middle and three defenders around the outside[cite: 23]. The attacker escapes through a gate for a point[cite: 23]. Defenders scan and move to block gates[cite: 23]. Change roles after 30 seconds or when the attacker gets three points[cite: 23].

activities.name: Tri-Zone Interception Box

pitch_diagram.image_description: A 20x10-yard vertical grid cleanly split into three fields: two wider outer boxes and a central horizontal track highlighted in yellow. A blue 'interceptor' player occupies the shaded middle track, while a second blue defender has stepped out high into the lower box to press. A yellow-striped 'passer' initiates a pass from the bottom baseline, attempting to find their yellow-striped partner waiting inside the far top zone. A small gray cone icon on the left touchline tracks the active score.

tactical_layout.dimensions: 20x10-yard field divided into three tracking zones[cite: 23]
tactical_layout.central_corridor: A highlighted horizontal middle lane containing defensive interceptors[cite: 23].
tactical_layout.player_positions: 2 central interceptors screening passing lanes exchanged between 2 outer targets[cite: 23].

activities.description: Create a 20x10-yard area with end zones and a middle zone[cite: 23]. Have two 'interceptors' in the middle zone with a 'passer' in each area between the zones[cite: 23]. An interceptor passes to a passer to start the game[cite: 23]. The passer needs to pass to their teammate, who then dribbles into the end zone for a point[cite: 23]. The interceptors try to block the pass[cite: 23]. One of them can also come out of the middle zone to press[cite: 23]. If they win the ball, they can break out to try to dribble to an end zone to score a point[cite: 23]. Play for up to five rounds then the teams change roles[cite: 23].

activities.name: Parallel Pitch Interception Progression

pitch_diagram.image_description: A composite visual detailing two parallel 20x15-yard match fields layout. The left field houses traditional shaded baseline end zones with active 3v3 configurations. The right field layout features a horizontal centerline and twin mini-goals protecting both baselines. A yellow indicator icon containing the letter 'i' is appended to the right baseline score box to highlight the added interception bonus mechanism.

tactical_layout.left_pitch_mechanics: Dribbling or passing into target end zones for standard scores[cite: 23].
tactical_layout.right_pitch_mechanics: A four-goal configuration integrating a specialized interception bonus reward[cite: 23].

activities.description: Create parallel 20x15-yard pitches for 3v3 or 4v4 games[cite: 23]. Have end zones on the first pitch[cite: 23]. Here, teams score a point for dribbling the ball into an end zone[cite: 23]. They get two points if they pass the ball into the zone for a teammate to control[cite: 23]. The receiver can only be in there for five seconds[cite: 23]. On the other pitch, play a four-goal game[cite: 23]. Put two mini-goals at each end with a goalkeeper[cite: 23]. Teams get a point for each goal they score[cite: 23]. If mini-goals aren't available, use cones to create gates for the keepers to protect[cite: 23]. To incentivise intercepting, and differentiate the practice from previous weeks, amend the scoring system[cite: 23]. Now, if the team out of possession intercept the ball and score during that phase of play, they gain a bonus point[cite: 23].

weekly_session_plans.theme: Be on guard to intercept revisited
objectives: Show intelligence when making decisions as defenders[cite: 23]
Add timing and deception skills when marking as an individual[cite: 23]
Understand the core skill of intercepting when defending in pairs and units[cite: 23]
coaching_points: Consolidate coaching points from the first two weeks[cite: 23].
When intercepting, judge whether it's possible by trying to see path of the ball and nearby attackers[cite: 23].
Decide to use 'best' surface for making first contact on the ball[cite: 23].
Execute an interception with a positive first touch[cite: 23].

activities.name: On Guard Gate Blocking (Revisited)

pitch_diagram.image_description: A complete structural matching diagram of the week 3 'on guard' activity. Features a 10x10-yard square area with 4 double-cone gates managed by three outer defenders trailing a central ball carrier.

tactical_layout.player_positions: 1 central attacker attempting to leak through 4 perimeter gates monitored by 3 outer scanning defenders[cite: 23].

activities.description: For 'on guard', set up four gates in a 10x10-yard area[cite: 23]. Have an attacker with a ball in the middle and three defenders around the outside[cite: 23]. The attacker escapes through a gate for a point[cite: 23]. Defenders scan and move to block gates[cite: 23]. Change roles after 30 seconds or when the attacker gets three points[cite: 23].

activities.name: Tri-Zone Interception Match (3v3 Variant)

pitch_diagram.image_description: A modification of the week 3 practice 1 diagram. The 20x10-yard field layout retains the horizontal central tracking lane. This time, the layout maps multiple players in the outer grids—showing two yellow-striped passers combining inside the bottom quadrant against a blue defender stepping up out of the central block, while two yellow-striped partners wait inside the opposite far third.

tactical_layout.player_positions: Expanded 3v3 structural configuration where multiple attackers combine to bypass a multi-player central interception line[cite: 23].

activities.description: Create a 20x10-yard area with end zones and a middle zone[cite: 23]. Have two interceptors in the middle zone with two passers in each half[cite: 23]. Or make it a 3v3 game[cite: 23]. Passers need to pass to a teammate in the other half, who then dribbles into the end zone for a point[cite: 23]. Interceptors try to block the pass[cite: 23]. One interceptor can come out of the middle zone to press[cite: 23]. If you have three interceptors, have two in the middle zone with the other pressing[cite: 23]. If they win the ball, they can break out to try to dribble to an end zone to score a point[cite: 23]. Play for up to five rounds then the teams change roles[cite: 23].

activities.name: Parallel Pitch Interception Progression (Revisited)

pitch_diagram.image_description: A matching visual layout of the week 3 practice 2 double-pitch framework. Integrates the standard 3v3 end-zone game alongside the four-goal game featuring the designated interception score bonus token.

tactical_layout.right_pitch_mechanics: Four-goal match setup utilizing the specialized 'i' score modifier[cite: 23].

activities.description: Create parallel 20x15-yard pitches for 3v3 or 4v4 games[cite: 23]. Have end zones on the first pitch[cite: 23]. Here, teams score a point for dribbling the ball into an end zone[cite: 23]. They get two points if they pass the ball into the zone for a teammate to control[cite: 23]. The receiver can only be in there for five seconds[cite: 23]. On the other pitch, play a four-goal game[cite: 23]. Put two mini-goals at each end with a goalkeeper[cite: 23]. Teams get a point for each goal they score[cite: 23]. If mini-goals aren't available, use cones to create gates for the keepers to protect[cite: 23]. To incentivise intercepting, and differentiate the practice from previous weeks, amend the scoring system[cite: 23]. Now, if the team out of possession intercept the ball and score during that phase of play, they gain a bonus point[cite: 23].

objectives: Demonstrate positive, enthusiastic, and intelligent defending[cite: 23]
Refine their skills and decision-making around marking and intercepting when defending as individuals, in pairs, and units[cite: 23]
Follow up winning the ball with positive actions[cite: 23]
coaching_points: Consolidate the coaching points of the previous four weeks[cite: 23].
Use previous learning to aid decision-making to skilfully mark, cover and intercept[cite: 23].

activities.name: Space Invaders Tag Box

pitch_diagram.image_description: A square 10x10-yard grid marked by four yellow corner cones. Inside the center of the box, a lone yellow-striped tagger tracks movements. Three blue outfield players are positioned on the outer perimeter lines, with horizontal and vertical white dashed tracks charting their paths as they attempt to sprint cleanly across the inner grid to the opposite side.

tactical_layout.player_positions: 1 active central tagger locked inside the grid vs. 3 outer runners attempting to cross the space without contact[cite: 23].

activities.description: Set up a 10x10-yard area using four cones[cite: 23]. Have a tagger inside the square with three other players on the outside[cite: 23]. Those players aim to get across to the opposite side of the area without being tagged[cite: 23]. The tagger gets a point per shoulder tag they make[cite: 23]. After three tags, the tagger swaps roles[cite: 23].

activities.name: Three-Pitch Activity Grid

pitch_diagram.image_description: A large macro-overview layout showing three distinct pitch templates aligned side-by-side to construct a rotational carousel framework. The left pitch details a flat match line, the middle details an open pitch, and the right field features a shaded multi-zone interception grid.

tactical_layout.structure: Three separate 20x15-yard training pitches combined into a single carousel network[cite: 23].
tactical_layout.rotation_interval: Teams rotate dynamically between the individual grids every 12-15 minutes[cite: 23].

activities.description: Set up three 20x15-yard pitches to play games from previous weeks[cite: 23]. Players will rotate around the carousel of activities every 12-15 minutes[cite: 23].

activities.name: Four-Goal Isolation Game (Pitch 1)

pitch_diagram.image_description: A vertical pitch grid split horizontally by a solid center line, featuring twin mini-goals on both baselines guarded by goalkeepers. Inside each separate half, an isolated 1v1 duel is active between a blue player and a yellow-striped player, forcing individual marking blocks.

tactical_layout.dimensions: 20x15-yard vertical match grid with a structural center line[cite: 23]
tactical_layout.player_positions: Parallel 1v1 isolation units locked inside their respective halves ahead of the goalkeepers[cite: 23].

activities.description: On pitch one it's the four-goal game[cite: 23]. Create a halfway line and lock players in to create a 1v1 in each half with a keeper guarding two mini-goals[cite: 23]. Players combine with their teammate and try to score[cite: 23]. When out of possession, the focus is on marking and intercepting to get the ball back to attack[cite: 23].

activities.name: End Zones Score & Tally Game (Pitch 2)

pitch_diagram.image_description: A vertical field featuring shaded baseline end zones. A blue player is shown completing a passing sequence into the target zone for a teammate, while a second diagram block depicts a blue player running outward past the sidelines to interact with a vertical whiteboard icon.

tactical_layout.dimensions: 20x15-yard vertical field featuring target baseline quadrants[cite: 23]
tactical_layout.scoring_mechanics: Passing or dribbling into the end zones triggers a requirement for the scorer to temporarily leave the field to record the goal on a whiteboard[cite: 23].

activities.description: Have end zones on pitch two[cite: 23]. Here, teams score a point for dribbling the ball into an end zone[cite: 23]. They get two points if they pass the ball into the zone for a teammate to control[cite: 23]. The receiver can only be in there for five seconds[cite: 23]. The scorer must then run to record the goal on a whiteboard[cite: 23].

activities.name: Tri-Zone Multi-Player Interception (Pitch 3)

pitch_diagram.image_description: A vertical field layout divided horizontally into three fields: two outer thirds and a highlighted yellow central track. Inside the central track, two blue interceptor players operate dynamically. Two yellow-striped passers combine inside the lower box to find their standalone partner in the far top box, while a grey tracker tracks the score on the sidelines.

tactical_layout.dimensions: 20x15-yard pitch divided horizontally into three zones[cite: 23]
tactical_layout.player_positions: 2 central interceptors locked inside the middle zone screening lines against multi-player outer attacking combinations[cite: 23].

activities.description: Have end zones and a middle zone on pitch three[cite: 23]. Two passers are locked in one half with one in the other[cite: 23]. Have two interceptors in the middle zone with a third allowed out to press[cite: 23]. Passers combine to get into an end zone to score[cite: 23]. Interceptors need to win the ball back then break out to score[cite: 23].

weekly_session_plans.theme: Front to back, defend to attack
objectives: Demonstrate positive, enthusiastic, and intelligent defending[cite: 23]
Refine their skills and decision-making around marking and intercepting when defending as individuals, in pairs, and units[cite: 23]
Follow up winning the ball with positive actions[cite: 23]
coaching_points: Consolidate the coaching points of the previous four weeks[cite: 23].
Use previous learning to aid decision-making to skilfully mark, cover and intercept[cite: 23].

activities.name: Asymmetric Low Block vs Target Squares

pitch_diagram.image_description: An tactical overview taking place across half of a full-sized field. At the top baseline, a full-sized goal box is guarded by a goalkeeper, protected by a large, compact low defensive block of eight blue outfield players. Facing them are seven yellow-striped attackers combining inside the final third. At the opposite far baseline (the halfway line), three separate small squares made of cones are lined up horizontally to act as target zones for the clearing defenders.

tactical_layout.equipment: 1 full-sized goal frame at the top baseline, 3 target boxes marked by yellow cones at the bottom baseline[cite: 23].
tactical_layout.player_positions: 8 blue defenders plus a keeper protecting the main goal vs. 7 yellow-striped attackers building an assault[cite: 23].

activities.description: On half a pitch, have a goal and a keeper at one end[cite: 23]. Put three squares at the other end[cite: 23]. The defending team has eight outfielders and a keeper and face seven attackers[cite: 23]. Defenders score a point for controlling the ball in any square[cite: 23]. The attackers score three points for scoring in the main goal[cite: 23]. Play offside for the team attacking the main goal but don't use it for the side attacking the squares[cite: 23]. After seven minutes, rotate the teams[cite: 23]. This then becomes an 8v8 for a round with a keeper always being on the side defending the main goal[cite: 23].

activities.name: Zonal Clearances & Counter Scoring

pitch_diagram.image_description: The identical half-pitch game layout from the primary phase. Horizontal dashed lines now cleanly divide the defenders' half into three distinct tactical thirds. Bright text indicators are overlaid near the target squares to denote a stratified scoring reward matrix: '3 points' at the deepest defensive third, '2 points' in the middle, and '1 point' at the edge of the final third zone.

tactical_layout.dimensions: Half-pitch layout segmented into three horizontal thirds[cite: 23]
tactical_layout.scoring_mechanics: Defenders receive stratified points (3, 2, or 1) based on the specific third where they win possession and initiate a clearance[cite: 23].

activities.description: Progress the game after two rounds[cite: 23]. Now, defenders get three points for winning the ball in their third and scoring[cite: 23]. Two for doing that from the middle[cite: 23]. And one for doing that from the final third[cite: 23]. The attackers get a bonus point if they intercept then score in the main goal[cite: 23].

activities.name: Score Tracker Asymmetry

pitch_diagram.image_description: The half-pitch game setup showing a yellow-striped attacker shooting past the blue defensive line. A small clipboard/whiteboard icon is placed adjacent to the main goal frame, with a dashed arrow showing the successful scorer running off the pitch to log the point.

tactical_layout.scoring_mechanics: Attackers who score must immediately leave the boundaries to record the goal on a whiteboard, creating a brief tactical overload for the defending team[cite: 23].

activities.description: After another two rounds, progress the game further by asking the scorer to run off the pitch to record their goal on a whiteboard[cite: 23]. This makes the scoring team a player down for a short time[cite: 23].

activities.name: Twin Pitch Small-Sided Breakdown

pitch_diagram.image_description: An alternative layout showing the half-pitch game broken down into two smaller, parallel pitches side-by-side. Each small field maintains a goal at one end and three target squares at the base, populated by downscaled 4v4 or 5v5 player configurations.

tactical_layout.target_groups: Specifically optimized for U12 and U13 development groups[cite: 23].

activities.description: This activity can also be played as two smaller games - particularly if you're working with U12s or U13s[cite: 23]. In this scenario, combine the scores from both pitches into one scoreline[cite: 23]. Also manage the rotation of players across both pitches if needed[cite: 23].
